meta.title: The Case for Solana and Internet Capital Markets

transcript: All righty. Good afternoon everyone. Thank you for being at Breakpoint 2025. Um, my name is Kyle Samani. I'm a founder and managing partner at Multicoin Capital. Uh, thank you for being here with me this afternoon. As some of the seed investors in Solana, we've had to re-underwrite our investment in SOL as it's gone up in price over the years. Earlier this year we went through that same process again, uh, and we concluded that Solana is still in the best position to be the dominant smart contract platform. Today, I'd like to talk about what undergirds our conviction. The short answer to that is Internet Capital Markets. Uh, but the longer and more nuanced answer that I'm going to talk about over the next 10 minutes, uh, is really why we think Solana has the core properties you need to power global scale Internet Capital Markets. Before I start, uh, you should read very fast. Um, here are some important disclosures for all of you. Cool. All right, so, uh, a couple of months ago, I gave a presentation at the All-In Summit, um, that laid out kind of the big idea behind Internet Capital Markets. In that presentation, uh, I described Internet Capital Markets as a system in which anyone can trade any asset anywhere in the world 24/7 with just a their phone and an internet connection. Uh, from an allocator's perspective, we think Internet Capital Markets is really one of the most important ideas happening in the world today, uh, up there with AI. But really no one has actually articulated kind of uh tried to answer the question, well what what are the core properties you need to make a system actually able to support Internet Capital Markets at global scale. And there are a lot of subtle design decisions that need to go into thinking about this and I want to answer those questions today. So we think there are basically four core properties you need in a system that is going to undergird Internet Capital Markets. And those four are Global Liquidity, Censorship Resistance, Credible Neutrality, uh and Jurisdictional Sovereignty. So let me jump into each of those. First is Global Liquidity. Um, this is pretty straightforward concept. Internet Capital Markets obviously should be able to tap into liquidity anywhere in the world. Um, but there's a little more nuance to think through beyond that. And I want to give an example to make that clear. Uh, let's say you live in the US and you want to buy stock A, uh, on-chain. And let's say there are three different trading venues on-chain. You have something like from the New York Stock Exchange that's on-chain. You have something from the London Stock Exchange that's on-chain. And let's say a permissionless DEX like Drift. And all three of those exchanges are trading stock A. Um, if you have the KYC credentials to trade on all three of those exchanges, then you should be able to tap into liquidity across all three of those venues and not just one. And by tapping into that liquidity, you should then receive the underlying asset back to your wallet instantly. Um, Internet Capital Markets are global, but the world is regional and we're going to have different venues on-chain. And we think it's super important to preserve this idea of of being global while still recognizing the reality of regional rules, jurisdictions, and KYC. Uh, and we think this global foundation Solana provides can actually accomplish that. Next property I want to touch on is Censorship Resistance. Um, there are obviously always going to be instances in which countries try and block people from doing things on-chain or accessing Internet Capital Markets. Uh, and obviously the more centralized a system is, uh, the easier it is to censor. Uh, decentralized systems are obviously much harder to to uh to censor. Uh, and crypto has really proven that we can actually have globally censorship resistant systems. Today, I think it's generally recognized that Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Solana at the very least are genuinely censorship resistant systems. Um, the key property of course that makes these systems censorship resistant is having block producers all over the world, uh, in many different jurisdictions and regions. Uh, and today of course across these different systems there are thousands of nodes producing blocks across more than 100 countries. Um, next next property I want to touch on is Credible Neutrality. This is probably the most subtle of these four properties and and maybe the hardest to appreciate. Uh, on the surface, uh, censorship resistance and credible neutrality uh appear to be fairly correlated and and they are. Um, uh, maybe the more nuanced way to think about it is that, um, the more censorship resistant a system is, the uh harder it is for any one actor to weaponize the system against other actors. Um, but the correlation between these properties starts to get a little fuzzier as you get into more nuanced situations. If you consider the Solana network today, Solana network today has about a thousand uh block producing nodes across 48 countries. Uh, there are 210 countries in the world, uh, and so that means obviously that not everyone in every country even has local representation there. But even if you did get, you know, at least one node per country, most of those nodes are going to have, you know, a tiny amount of stake. Uh, and we think it's fairly unlikely that if you are a regulator in uh a smaller country, that you're going to look at a system in which you have 0.1% or 0.01% stake and think that's credibly neutral to the citizens of your country and jurisdiction. Um, and so this idea of credible neutrality, uh, it's easy for us to take for granted in some of the major metros, but uh much harder as you get to smaller uh environments. Uh, and then the fourth property I want to touch on, uh, is kind of this new idea that we look at called Jurisdictional and Application Sovereignty. Um, on the surface this property seems a little bit at odds with the prior three which are all kind of intrinsically global in nature. Um, but I don't think it has to be. Uh, for anyone who studied the history of financial markets and exchanges, you know that there is not one correct construction for market microstructure. You have um taker speed bumps, you can have maker cancel pri maker cancel priority, you can have maker rebates, you can have priority size liquid priority liquidations. There's all kinds of mechanics you can play with in different microstructure designs. And if you look across the world at the major trading venues today across equities, commodities, FX, and others, you see that there is not one uniform standard for that. Um, well if you have a single global system, you know, how are you going to reconcile these different microstructure realities against one global system? And so we believe that, you know, in order to have Internet Capital Markets, you need to be able to respect the needs of regulators and uh exchange providers in these jurisdictions. Uh, and so the next question is, well, you know, how are you actually going to achieve all of these objectives um in one system today because as of today, no no blockchain in the world does all of the above, but I think the folks at Solana are working on it. We think there are fundamentally two major technologies that are now being worked on that we think unlock all of the above. And those are MCL, uh Multiple Concurrent Leaders, and Application-Controlled Execution or ACE. If this is your first Breakpoint, uh MCL was actually introduced by Anatoly two years ago, uh and ACE was actually introduced last year. Uh, we think these two uh core ideas which are now both under active development from Solana core engineering are going to be the the two things that really facilitate Internet Capital Markets at truly truly global scale. Uh, so let me explain that. Uh, today almost all major blockchains are single leader systems, whether they're L1s or L2s, EVM, SVM, or something else. Um, and what that means is that at any one moment in time, there is one block producer somewhere in the world that has a monopoly on block production. Um, this uh yeah. Uh, the premise of Multiple Concurrent Leaders is actually pretty straightforward. It's of course the idea of having multiple concurrent you know people producing blocks at the same time. Um, the the reason this is important for things like credible neutrality in particular and censorship resistance is that it allows you to kind of multiply effective stake. Um, if you have a system where you have a hundred block producing nodes at the same time, uh and you have a 0.1% stake in that global network, that would be the equivalent of having 10% stake in a single leader system like Solana is today. Uh, and so we think that the ability to have, you know, way way more concurrent leaders is going to allow there to be more nodes in more places, which is ultimately going to make regulators and application developers in all of these long tail jurisdictions feel comfortable trusting this global system. Uh, the second major technical requirement we think that's going to be key to unlock this vision is Application-Controlled Execution, uh otherwise known as ACE. Uh, on the surface it would appear that a single integrated system like Solana uh is really antithetical to this idea. Uh, and this is why Application-Controlled Execution is really necessary. Um, ACE will allow developers to specify uh custom ordering rules. Things like maker prioritization, taker speed bumps, maker cancels, priority liquidation, all these different things, all while still being on one global system with one global liquidity pool in the way that Solana is today. Um, ACE is really allows uh uh regulators and exchange operators, whether you're the London Stock Exchange, the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, or anyone else, to define their own custom rules while still tapping into the benefits of Solana as a global system. Uh, and we think this is super underappreciated. Uh, to recap, we think Internet Capital Markets are going to require four um properties to really get to global scale. Uh, and those are of course Global Liquidity, Censorship Resistance, Credible Neutrality, uh and Jurisdictional and Application Specific Sovereignty. Um, we think if you look today at kind of the designs of these systems, the only one that's actually going to get you there is Solana. Ethereum, uh, you know, maybe is credibly neutral, uh it's definitely censorship resistant, it definitely provides global liquidity. Um, but you look at the L2s, you know, they don't really achieve any of the first three objectives. Uh, and we think that this is this is going to be the path that's going to truly get global buy-in from heterogeneous participants uh all over the world. Uh, well we think Solana is really building the most ambitious vision for the future of Internet Capital Markets. Um, and that's why we're so excited about where Solana is uh and where it's going in the years to come. So thank you all.

Nov 18, 2025 9:30 AM Eastern Standard Time
# **Canary Capital Launches Canary Marinade Solana ETF (SOLC), Giving Investors Simple Access to Solana and Staking Rewards Potential**
Share
*SOLC provides a cost-effective and hands-off way to access price appreciation and staking potential, unlocking a new yield-distributing strategy for a modern portfolio*
BRENTWOOD, Tenn.--([BUSINESS WIRE](https://www.businesswire.com))--[Canary Capital Group LLC](https://cts.businesswire.com/ct/CT?id=smartlink&url=https://www.canary.capital/&esheet=54359654&newsitemid=20251118770018&lan=en-US&anchor=Canary+Capital+Group+LLC&index=1&md5=9bb7478aca5e4beebf53fbb62385830e)(“Canary Capital”), a digital asset investment management firm, today announced the launch of the[Canary Marinade Solana ETF](https://cts.businesswire.com/ct/CT?id=smartlink&url=https://etfs.canary.capital/solc/&esheet=54359654&newsitemid=20251118770018&lan=en-US&anchor=Canary+Marinade+Solana+ETF&index=2&md5=d79fabdbfe8445597e6b80129e657211)(NASDAQ: SOLC), an exchange-traded product designed to provide investors with spot exposure to Solana (SOL) the native cryptocurrency behind one of the world’s fastest growing

tags.impact_reason: Keynote from Kyle Samani of Multicoin Capital argues Solana is best positioned to power Internet Capital Markets, outlining core properties needed for global scale.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a Crystalline Architectural Core as the Semantic Anchor because the story is fundamentally about Solana's 'architecture' and 'core properties' acting as the foundational infrastructure for 'Global Internet Capital Markets'. A massive, high-tech monolith visually represents this powerful, central, and unified system. The internal, glowing data streams symbolize the 'Global Liquidity' flowing through it. For the homepage, I included the diegetic text 'GLOBAL' as a holographic system display on the core's surface, reinforcing the central thesis of a unified, worldwide financial market.
visual_idea.description: A monumental, crystalline monolith, representing the Solana architecture, stands in a vast, dark, and minimalist data hall. The structure is made of polished, semi-translucent obsidian, with glowing cyan and gold data streams pulsing within its geometric core, visualizing global liquidity. A faint, holographic system projection on its surface reads 'GLOBAL'. The background is deep and atmospheric, with the subtle outlines of distant server architecture creating a sense of immense scale. The lighting is sharp and cinematic, casting clean highlights on the monolith's facets.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A monumental, crystalline monolith, representing the Solana architecture, stands in a vast, dark, and minimalist data hall, emphasizing height and epic scale, with ample negative space above. The structure is made of polished, semi-translucent obsidian, reflecting sharp, cinematic highlights and casting subtle, soft gradient shadows, showcasing exceptional detail and intricate textures. Glowing cyan and gold data streams pulse vibrantly within its geometric core, visualizing global liquidity. A faint, holographic system projection on its surface reads 'GLOBAL', appearing as an integrated part of the monolith's display. The background is deep and atmospheric, with the subtle outlines of distant server architecture creating a sense of immense scale, gradually softening into an infinite soft background. Lighting is clean studio-quality, using soft commercial product lighting with global illumination, a large diffused neutral white (5500K) softbox key light, and a white reflector fill light, ensuring even light distribution and ambient occlusion, with sharp reflections on the polished obsidian surface. 8k resolution, ray-traced optics, physically based rendering (PBR), octane render, hyper-detailed.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a Crystalline Architectural Core as the Semantic Anchor because the story is fundamentally about Solana's 'architecture' and 'core properties' acting as the foundational infrastructure for 'Global Internet Capital Markets'. A massive, high-tech monolith visually represents this powerful, central, and unified system. The internal, glowing data streams symbolize the 'Global Liquidity' flowing through it. For the homepage, I included the diegetic text 'GLOBAL' as a holographic system display on the core's surface, reinforcing the central thesis of a unified, worldwide financial market.
visual_idea.description: A macro shot focusing on the corner of the crystalline Solana monolith. The camera is tight on the polished obsidian surface, revealing the incredible detail of the glowing, intricate data pathways just beneath it. A primary artery of liquid gold light flows diagonally across the frame, with smaller, thread-like cyan circuits branching off. The image emphasizes the complex internal structure and raw power of the underlying infrastructure. NO TEXT.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A precisely framed, symmetrical macro shot, focused intensely on the corner of a crystalline Solana monolith. The camera is tight on the highly polished obsidian surface, gleaming under soft commercial product lighting (global illumination), revealing the incredible, intricate detail of glowing holographic data pathways just beneath its surface. A primary artery of ethereal liquid gold light flows diagonally across the square 1:1 frame, brilliantly illuminated by ray-traced optics, with smaller, thread-like cyan circuits branching off, evoking an architectural blueprint. The entire frame is filled with this rich, complex internal structure, emphasizing the raw power of the underlying infrastructure. Clean studio lighting with even light distribution and ambient occlusion highlights the depths and luminous elements, creating a cyberpunk glow with a soft ambient light. 8k resolution, octane render, physically based rendering (PBR), hyper-detailed.

editorial.headline: Multicoin Capital: Solana Architecture Unlocks Global Internet Capital Markets
editorial.standfirst: Multicoin Capital re-underwrites its high-conviction bet on Solana, arguing that specific architectural upgrades now reconcile global liquidity with local regulatory sovereignty. The firm asserts that upcoming protocol evolutions render the fragmented 'app-chain' thesis obsolete for institutional finance.
key_takeaways: Multicoin Capital identifies Multiple Concurrent Leaders (MCL) as the mechanism to multiply 'effective stake,' granting smaller nations credible neutrality.
Application-Controlled Execution (ACE) allows venues like the London Stock Exchange to enforce custom market microstructures on the public mainnet.
The analysis posits that Ethereum L2s fail the 'global liquidity' and 'credible neutrality' tests required for a unified financial layer.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
The vision of Internet Capital Markets (ICM) requires solving a paradox: maintaining a single global liquidity state that somehow respects fragmented local jurisdictions. **Multicoin Capital** posits that the current blockchain landscape is bifurcated between the fragmented liquidity of **Ethereum** L2s and the rigid global state of monolithic chains. The alpha lies in Solana’s roadmap to bridge this gap through **Multiple Concurrent Leaders (MCL)**. By allowing simultaneous block production, Solana aims to multiply "effective stake," giving smaller jurisdictions and regulators the mathematical assurance of credible neutrality without fracturing the network into sovereign silos.

Institutional adoption hinges on market microstructure, not just throughput. The deployment of **Application-Controlled Execution (ACE)** represents a pivot from "raw speed" to "customizable fairness." This feature allows venues—whether the **London Stock Exchange** or a permissionless DEX—to implement specific ordering rules like maker rebates, priority liquidations, or taker speed bumps directly on the L1. This effectively neutralizes the argument for permissioned app-chains; institutions can now retain sovereignty over their execution logic while tapping into **Solana's** unified global liquidity pool.

The analysis concludes that the "modular" thesis touted by the **Ethereum** ecosystem fails the requirements of a true ICM. While Ethereum L1 possesses censorship resistance, its L2 ecosystem fractures liquidity and centralizes sequencing, destroying credible neutrality. **Solana** stands alone in attempting to scale a single state machine that satisfies the four pillars: Global Liquidity, Censorship Resistance, Credible Neutrality, and Jurisdictional Sovereignty. For allocators, this signals that the infrastructure layer war is shifting from "speed" to "sovereign flexibility" within a shared state.
